---

### ✅ Story 21-1: Extract `workflow-actors.ts` — All ACs Verified

| AC | Check | Result |
|----|-------|--------|
| AC1 | `npm run build` exits 0 | ✅ |
| AC2 | 4976 tests, 0 failures | ✅ |
| AC3 | `workflow-actors.ts` = **199 lines** (≤ 200) | ✅ |
| AC4 | `workflow-machine.ts` = **1107 lines** (≤ 1276) | ✅ |
| AC5 | 0 back-references to `workflow-machine` in actors | ✅ |
| AC6 | Import from `./workflow-actors.js` present in machine | ✅ |
| AC7 | 140 dispatch tests pass (11 files) | ✅ |
| AC8 | 254 null-task tests pass (66 files) | ✅ |
| AC9 | ESLint on `workflow-actors.ts` — 0 errors/warnings | ✅ |
| AC10 | `tsc --noEmit` — 0 errors referencing `workflow-actors` | ✅ |

**Sprint status:** `in-progress` → `review`

The extraction is a pure move — `dispatchTaskCore`, `nullTaskCore`, `dispatchActor`, `nullTaskDispatchActor`, and helpers were moved to `workflow-actors.ts` with `workflow-constants.ts` and `workflow-types.ts` absorbing constants/interfaces to stay under the 200-line budget. No behavior changes, no regressions.

💡 **Tip:** Run `code-review` with a different LLM for peer review of this story.
